qa_pairs.question: What does an SSH public key look like?
qa_pairs.answer: An SSH public key typically appears as a very long single line, similar to this example: `ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQABAAACAQC3qeojDkUShnPTq9pI3cCZe+jgD6RKA/6CPsJIWZ8MqbX6wkk6 hHgKqKd2/9d7cj8e03Cfv4JLoD++P9fUPE3UyYrP/uVi4zytp5rmIZI4Qo1LvD1Obs0e78y0Dp7pfG1EHTYdn0p8 zHa0lNLOrZMmzDP0UMVdf4WKuv3Th2K/35yQ7DVIei6X/33Dcmsqd8bTXq7aFlw2y4sa/CHs314G6WYuJ9cTXtsW Dlc9oWJuOVqILJLeGQpl3BVEM9aKcYksqLMV1UlZF8bHbry0PKCnrJrNMzVulWfnmSOZ+lPcV32OsDRkFaKsdxPy +PxywieC86mxy1v216jeOdHnhLfOJc/VYDqf4egxReSCb3WOucHBB5J1jtDt47GuamKs+F2T7obqCb0J6oTyzgVF RIZryxwvh5fQF5jk3LsBGsbhe9GYwDAk54GV6I0rWnXp56mJZjO4JCRQGbwLAJVxH4a7UrBmba2pRcZxEZmbIcBB Sjb9KPECtaxiY/aty39077DCmcLCmzeOgBdh0zIkdBYu+OJ65MFKMxzoJWPDbZIcbSRGHEVhDnBZMNj1OiJS+E2D A+F0tPH7J+xox1vUoXGAI0cNv+s/nlVRuOZoZjhk6s7tLXeVcToc+Y9Wqx8fdL7D4FegWwB9lsVhpfC4NaA9R8Ao OfJUwHSNqUc6SfIt7w== user@machine`.

qa_pairs.question: Can I use the example SSH public key provided in the document?
qa_pairs.answer: No, you must generate your own pair of public and private keys; you cannot copy the example provided for your own use.
